story.iWant: to choose whether API endpoint specs are generated for my ticket
story.soThat: non-API tickets (UI-only changes, config changes, docs) don't include irrelevant endpoint definitions

tasks.task: Add API toggle state to generation wizard store Add `includeApiSpec: boolean` field to `generation-wizard.store.ts` Add `apiContext: string` field Initialize defaults based on ticket type in `setType()` action Add `setIncludeApiSpec()`, `setApiContext()` actions

Add API toggle card to GenerationOptionsStep Add second toggle card to `GenerationOptionsStep.tsx` (created in 14.1) Side-by-side layout on desktop, stacked on mobile Progressive disclosure: show API context input only when toggle is ON Text field with placeholder for API description No image upload for API (text-only context)
Wire API preferences through wizard Pass `includeApiSpec` and `apiContext` to `confirmContextContinue()` Ensure the `detect-apis` call is skipped when `includeApiSpec=false`

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Given a user on the generation options step When the step renders Then an "Include API Endpoint Spec?" toggle card is shown alongside the wireframe toggle, with smart default based on ticket type (ON for `feature`, OFF for `bug` and `task`)
Given the API toggle is ON When the user expands the context section Then they see a text field ("Describe endpoints, methods, payloads...") for optional API context hints
Given the API toggle is OFF When the spec is finalized Then `techSpec.apiChanges` is empty/null and no API endpoint definitions appear in the generated spec
Given the user provides API context text When the spec is finalized Then the LLM receives this context and produces more accurate endpoint specifications
Given both toggles are OFF When the user proceeds Then the wizard skips directly to draft generation without generating wireframes or API specs

doc.title: Epic 14: Optional Wireframe and API Generation
doc.snippet: Defines the API toggle acceptance criteria: toggle Yes/No with type-based defaults, context text input, conditional exclusion of apiChanges in techSpec output.

doc.title: Story 14.1: Optional Wireframe/UI Generation Step
doc.snippet: Sibling story that creates GenerationOptionsStep.tsx component with wireframe toggle. Story 14.2 extends this same component with an API toggle card.

doc.title: Story 14.3: Backend Conditional Spec Generation
doc.snippet: Downstream story that modifies CreateTicketDto and FinalizeSpecUseCase to conditionally include/exclude API sections based on includeApiSpec flag. Story 14.2 wires the frontend toggle; 14.3 handles backend logic.

file.reason: Primary modification target. Must add includeApiSpec:boolean, apiContext:string to WizardState; add setIncludeApiSpec(), setApiContext() to WizardActions; initialize defaults in setType(); pass values through confirmContextContinue().

Container component orchestrating wizard stages. May need to render GenerationOptionsStep at the appropriate stage and pass API preferences. Currently renders Stage1Input, Stage2ReproSteps, Stage3Draft.
Reference for existing wizard step pattern — input fields, store bindings, layout conventions.
Reference for how confirmContextContinue() is invoked and how wizard transitions to draft generation stage.
May need update if GenerationOptionsStep adds a new visible stage to the wizard flow indicator.
The detect-apis endpoint (POST :id/detect-apis) that should be conditionally skipped when includeApiSpec=false. Understanding its current invocation pattern is needed for Task 3.
Service that performs API detection from file contents. Called by detectApis controller method. Skipping this call is the backend optimization when API spec is disabled.
Will need includeApiSpec and apiContext fields added (story 14.3 scope, but 14.2 must send compatible data).

package.name: tailwindcss
package.reason: Styling — responsive grid for side-by-side cards

constraint.rule: Business logic MUST live in Zustand store actions — NEVER in React hooks or components. The toggle state, defaults logic, and conditional API call skipping belong in the store.
UI must remain minimalistic and calm (Linear-inspired). Toggle cards should use progressive disclosure — API context input only visible when toggle is ON.
Atomic Design: GenerationOptionsStep is an organism (feature-level UI block). Toggle cards are molecules.
ALL card borders MUST use `border border-[var(--border-subtle)]` — never hardcoded border-white/[0.XX].
No hidden side effects. Store actions must be deterministic and testable.
Strong typing: avoid `any`. The includeApiSpec and apiContext fields must have explicit types.
This story extends GenerationOptionsStep created in 14.1. Two toggle cards share the same step.
Smart defaults: Feature -> ON, Bug -> OFF, Task -> OFF. Same pattern as wireframes but independently controlled.
API context is text-only (no image upload). Unlike wireframes which accept image uploads.
detect-apis endpoint (POST /tickets/{aecId}/detect-apis) should be conditionally called only when includeApiSpec=true.

interface.name: WizardState
interface.signature: interface WizardState { includeApiSpec: boolean; apiContext: string; /* ...existing fields */ }

interface.name: WizardActions
interface.signature: interface WizardActions { setIncludeApiSpec: (include: boolean) => void; setApiContext: (context: string) => void; /* ...existing actions */ }

tests.standards: Backend use-case tests use Jest with `.spec.ts` suffix, located alongside source files in the use-cases directory. Tests mock infrastructure adapters (repositories, services) and test use-case behavior directly. Client-side tests are not yet established in the tickets module — store actions should be testable by mocking authFetch and verifying state transitions.

ideas.idea: Verify that setType('feature') sets includeApiSpec=true and setType('bug')/setType('task') sets includeApiSpec=false
Verify GenerationOptionsStep renders API toggle card with correct default state based on ticket type
Verify API context text field appears only when includeApiSpec toggle is ON (progressive disclosure)
Verify that when includeApiSpec=false, the detect-apis call is not made during confirmContextContinue
Verify that apiContext string is included in the request body when both includeApiSpec=true and apiContext is non-empty
Verify that when both includeWireframes=false and includeApiSpec=false, wizard proceeds directly to draft generation
